title: Cross-Research-Analyst parallel synthesis — TrimTab 3B Velocity Prediction Optimization
findings: R²=0.688 ceiling is primarily from layer depth (36 layers) compounding prediction errors, not quantization noise
Global per-dimension normalization destroys layer-specific signal structure — per-layer normalization expected +0.08-0.12 R²
NTFS I/O bottleneck (0.023 ep/s) is the highest-leverage operational intervention with 10-20× speedup potential
Last 8-10 layers have negative R² contribution — skipping them improves overall prediction
Ensemble of 3 small TTs (d_model=512) likely beats single large TT (d_model=1280) due to attention O(d²) scaling + per-layer variance exploitation
conclusions: Implement per-layer normalization first (P0) — highest expected R² gain at lowest effort
Migrate data from NTFS to SSD immediately (P0) — gates all other optimization speed
Add per-layer R² monitoring to expose representational degradation (P0) — prevents wasted sessions
further_avenues: FA-1: Controlled fp16 vs 4-bit precision comparison (quantifies noise ceiling)
FA-2: PCA/EOF decomposition + Neural ODE in latent space
FA-3: 3B TT on first 24 layers only (tests layer-count hypothesis)
next_steps: P0: Implement per-layer normalization in run_train_gen_tt_3b.py
P0: Copy data from NTFS to SSD + memory-map for 10-20× training speedup
P0: Add per-layer R² monitoring to detect terminal-layer collapse
P1: Skip last 10 degraded layers (experiment with layers 0-25)
P2: Train 3× d_model=512 ensemble with layer-wise confidence weighting
